title: The wrapper that was missing
testCase: Did you wrap the content in <html>, <head> and <body>?
blocks.0.text: So far you have written bare text with tags, and it worked. The browser is forgiving — it swallows whatever you give it and tries to show it.
blocks.1.text: But a real page has a wrapper. And it is not a formality.
blocks.4.text: <html> wraps everything — this is the document itself. Inside it there are exactly two things, and they are completely different in meaning.
blocks.6.text: <body> is the page a person sees. Everything you want to appear on screen goes here. Headings, sentences, images — everything.
blocks.7.text: <head> is the opposite: nothing in it is visible. It holds information ABOUT the page — what it is called, what language it is in, who wrote it. Like a label on a box: not part of the contents, but it tells you what is inside.
blocks.9.text: Take your page and wrap it properly: <html> on the outside, <head> empty for now, and everything visible inside <body>.
blocks.10.text: Run. Nothing will change on screen. That is exactly why it was easy to skip — and exactly why you must get used to writing it.
